published_title: Trade, Endogenous Quality, and Welfare in Motion Pictures
authors: Fernando Ferreira; Amil Petrin; Joel Waldfogel
note: Consistently cited as 'Trade, Endogenous Quality, and Welfare in Motion Pictures,' Mimeo/working paper, University of Minnesota, 2012. Checked Petrin research page (403), Waldfogel Oct-2019 CV (binary, unreadable), Google Scholar and multiple citations - no peer-reviewed journal issue found. Remains an unpublished working paper. Authors later produced a distinct related 2016 WP ('Preference Externalities and the Rise of China...').
Published in Journal of Public Economics, vol. 125 (2015), pp. 98-115. Confirmed via ScienceDirect and RePEc (eee/pubeco/v125y2015icp98-115).

published_title: Search Frictions and Market Power in Negotiated-Price Markets
authors: Jason Allen; Robert Clark; Jean-Francois Houde
note: Conference paper (also NBER WP 19883) published as 'Search Frictions and Market Power in Negotiated-Price Markets,' Journal of Political Economy vol. 127(4), 2019, pp. 1550-1598. Confirmed via University of Chicago Press page (DOI 10.1086/701684) and RePEc.

published_title: Are Supermarkets Squeezing Small Suppliers? Evidence from Negotiated Wholesale Prices
note: Agenda listed only presenter Noton; coauthor Andres Elberg recovered. Working paper 'Revealing Bargaining Power through Actual Wholesale Prices' published as 'Are Supermarkets Squeezing Small Suppliers? Evidence from Negotiated Wholesale Prices,' The Economic Journal vol. 128(610), May 2018, pp. 1304-1330. Confirmed via RePEc/EconPapers.

Published in RAND Journal of Economics, vol. 56(4), 2025, pp. 444-475 (DOI 10.1111/1756-2171.70011, Wiley page paywalled/402 but confirmed via search + author working-paper draft SeimVitorino2024). Conference agenda also listed David Muir; published version authored by Seim and Vitorino only.

published_title: Repairing the Damage: The Effect of Price Knowledge and Gender on Auto Repair Price Quotes
authors: Meghan R. Busse; Ayelet Israeli; Florian Zettelmeyer
note: Retitled from 'Repairing the Damage: The Effect of Price Expectations...' Published in Journal of Marketing Research vol. 54(1), Feb 2017, pp. 75-95 (DOI 10.1509/jmr.13.0291). Confirmed via SAGE and Northwestern/HBS listings.

published_title: Medicare Part D: Are Insurers Gaming the Low Income Subsidy Design?
note: Published as 'Medicare Part D: Are Insurers Gaming the Low Income Subsidy Design?' American Economic Review vol. 105(4), 2015, pp. 1547-1580 (DOI 10.1257/aer.20130903). Confirmed via AEA and RePEc (aecrev/v105y2015i4p1547-80).

published_title: Financial Constraints and Moral Hazard: The Case of Franchising
authors: Ying Fan; Kai-Uwe Kuhn; Francine Lafontaine
note: Retitled from 'Financial Constraints and Franchising Decisions.' Published as 'Financial Constraints and Moral Hazard: The Case of Franchising,' Journal of Political Economy vol. 125(6), 2017, pp. 2082-2125 (DOI 10.1086/694566). Confirmed via UChicago Press.

authors: Soren T. Anderson; Ryan Kellogg; Stephen W. Salant
note: Published (also NBER WP 20280) in Journal of Political Economy vol. 126(3), 2018, pp. 984-1026 (DOI 10.1086/697203). Confirmed via UChicago Press and RePEc.

Conference title 'Optimal Regulation with Exemptions and Corrective Taxes' shortened to 'Optimal Regulation with Exemptions.' Published in International Journal of Industrial Organization vol. 66, 2019 (also NBER WP 23887). Confirmed via ScienceDirect.

authors: Aviv Nevo; John L. Turner; Jonathan W. Williams
note: Published in Econometrica vol. 84(2), March 2016, pp. 411-443 (DOI 10.3982/ECTA11927; also NBER WP 21321). Confirmed via Wiley/Econometric Society and RePEc.

published_title: The Unfavorable Economics of Measuring the Returns to Advertising
authors: Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao
note: Retitled from 'On the Near Impossibility of Measuring the Returns to Advertising.' Published as 'The Unfavorable Economics of Measuring the Returns to Advertising,' Quarterly Journal of Economics vol. 130(4), 2015, pp. 1941-1973. Confirmed via search results.

Only found as SSRN working paper (abstract 2309622, 2014) and AEA conference version. Checked Tappata's ResearchGate/Google Scholar research page - no journal publication, forthcoming, or R&R located for this specific paper (his forthcoming REStat paper is a different, cross-border-travel project). Remains a working paper.

published_title: How Do Electricity Shortages Affect Industry? Evidence from India
authors: Hunt Allcott; Allan Collard-Wexler; Stephen D. O'Connell
note: Title changed 'Productivity' -> 'Industry.' Published as 'How Do Electricity Shortages Affect Industry? Evidence from India,' American Economic Review vol. 106(3), 2016, pp. 587-624 (also NBER WP 19977). Confirmed via AEA.
